desc:
This wide road is bordered to both sides by large structures, but every entrance
has been blocked or totally destroyed. The remnants of these acts of destruction
have left a fine film of dust everywhere, and small stones and debris litter the
road, which continues to the east and west.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
A wide road running east west is cut by a small passage going north. To the
south it broadens out into a small plaza. There is a very malodorous stench
drifting in from the north.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
This rough tunnel seems to have been added on at a much later date than the
tunnel to the south. The work looks to have been done by much rougher hands and
accomplished with much less skill. The walls are quite rough and the footing can
be quite tricky. It continues to the north and dumps onto a much more polished
hallway to the south.
name: Stinking Passage

desc:
This passage looks to be of much newer make than the one to the immediate north.
The handiwork and craftsmanship is of a much lesser skill level. The walls are
neither smooth nor particularly straight and sweep almost drunkenly to the
south.
name: Stinking Passage

desc:
This rather plain hallway continues to the west but slopes rather sharply
upward. The hallway, while plain, brings out the natural grain of the rock. This
explains the rather steep slope as a striation of the rock juts up at a steep
descent.
name: Hallway of Aule

desc:
This rather plain hallway runs both east and west. The natural beauty of the
rock has been brought out in the simple yet skilful craftsmanship of the tunnel,
which has not a single corner or angle to be seen. All is elegantly curved, and
finely polished.
name: Hallway of Aule

desc:
A wide avenue opens up to the east and a small ramp slopes down to a lower
level. Small shops and what looks like the fronts of houses line the avenue.
Most of the entrances have been reduced to rubble or otherwise sealed off. The
corridor is lit by a myriad of gems and jewels each catching and refracting
whatever available light there is.
name: Avenue of Stars

desc:
The Avenue extends in both directions. The destruction on either side of the
Avenue is almost complete. All doors and windows have been sealed or completely
destroyed. Hundreds of gems embedded in the stone catch and refract any
available light.
name: Avenue of Stars

desc:
The destruction in this part of the Avenue is almost complete. Several large
blocks which made up a nearby wall of a house encroach upon the Avenue making
travel tight in places.
name: Avenue of Stars

desc:
The destruction here looks different then other parts of the Avenue. The rock
seems scored and gouged in countless places. Dark red and black marks litter the
floor and walls. High above a mosaic sits in pristine preservation making a
marked contrast to the rest of the room.
name: Avenue of Stars

desc:
The large hallway coming from the east ends in a set of stairs leading
downwards. There is a large mosaic covering the whole ceiling above your head.
It is comprised of hundreds of reflective pieces of glass. It seems to have
survived largely intact. Probably due to the height of the ceiling.
name: Avenue of Stars

desc:
The road makes a straight path through to the east and west. Blacker holes to
the west indicate some passages breaking off of the main road. The sound of
water gurgling somewhere off in the distance breaks the silence.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
The road heads off to the west. The rough pavement of the road gives way to
smoothly polished marble to the east as the road gives way to a large vaulted
hall.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
The hall vaults up to a lofty one hundred foot height. There are thick black
columns of polished marble which hold up quadripartite vaults. Each vault
contains a bas-relief in dwarven history. Clever air holes add natural shadowing
to the artwork.
name: Hall of Lore

desc:
The ceiling swoops up to about one hundred feet and is supported by several
massive pillars each about fifteen feet around. Several carvings still survive
suspended about 75 feet up and filling the spaces between the pillars. The
ceiling of the hall is decorated with frescoes and carvings which cunningly mask
several airshafts.
name: Hall of Lore

desc:
The Hall is quite impressive soaring to a height of about one hundred feet. Huge
pillars ring the walls of the hall supporting the ceiling. Several cracks have
developed under these pillars however and they form a fine patina of cracks that
run from pillar to pillar. There is a small exit going north and a large
fountain is directly south.
name: Hall of Lore

desc:
The hall comes to a corner here while extending to the south and west. Huge
pillars about fifteen feet in diameter soar up a stunning one hundred feet to
support the ceiling. Large tapestries have been hung strung between the pillars
about seventy five feet up.
name: Hall of Lore

desc:
The hall vaults up to a lofty one hundred foot height. There are thick black
columns of polished marble which hold up quadripartite vaults. Each vault
contains cleverly carved scenes of dwarven history. A wide set of stairs opens
up to the east.
name: Hall of Lore

desc:
Columns ring the entire hall. Huge black monoliths raising up and touching the
ceiling. Blocks of quarts act like huge mirrors set in the ceiling magnifying
the available light.
name: Hall of Lore

desc:
Columns of shiny black marble support the ceiling. You can see several airshafts
arround the hall that allow the daylight to peek in and light the room. Blocks
of quartz are used to reflect the light towards the centre of the hall. There is
a large fountain in the middle of the hall, just north of here. A dark tunnel
exits south.
name: Hall of Lore

desc:
You are standing in the south western corner of the Hall of Lore. The ceiling is
resting on huge pillars about a hundred feet above your head. Each and every
sound is hurled back and forth between the walls and the ceiling, making a
rumbling echo that reveals your position to everyone in your vicinity.
name: Hall of Lore

desc:
In the middle of the hall there is a small pool providing Durin's Mansion with
water from the Mirrormere. The water pours out from a tall statue in the middle
of the pool. Both the fountain and the pool are cut right out of the mountain
and, like the floor, no cracks can be seen. The water in the pool is filthy and
looks rather disgusting, but the water coming from the fountain looks fresh.
name: Pool of Kheled-zaram

desc:
The walls of this avenue are covered in scribbling. Most of it has succumbed to
the vagaries of time and chance. Some newer writing is evident and legible. A
small stairway heads down. The avenue continues to the south while a large door
occupies the east wall.
name: Avenue of Balin

desc:
A wide set of shallow steps greeted by a low slung handrail on either side. The
handrail consists of small squat columns capped by a wide hand rail worn smooth
by thousands of hands. The steps once were covered by a carpet, but all that is
left is scraps of fabric and a slight indent in the centre of the stairs
presumably where the bulk of traffic went.
name: Lore Stairs

desc:
The road continues on drearily to the south but opens into a magnificent hall to
the north. There is little adornment on the walls or ceiling and the road seems
to have been designed for more practical uses.
name: A Dwarven Road

desc:
The road goes ever on to the north and south. It is wide enough for two carts to
pass with some difficulty. The flagstones are in decent condition with just a
few here and there cracked or missing. There is little adornment, just solid
dwarven stonework.
name: Dwarven Road

desc:
A wide path that has been covered in cobblestones. The natural rock can be seen
peeking up where the cobblestones have given way to decay or forcibly removed.
The walls are smooth and straight, broken only by an alcove recessed straight
into the rock every twenty feet or so. Most of the alcoves only hold the
shattered remnants of once stood there, but occasionally a leg or a marble piece
of a torso is still recognizable. In the centre is a statue of a dwarf, staring
back almost disconsolately at the destruction that has occured around him.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
The wide cobblestone pavement shows signs of water wear here. The mortar holding
the cobblestones together has turned a dark shade of green where the water has
been allowed to linger. Alcoves spaced every twenty feet extend down the
corridor on either side. Most seem to be empty or hold only the shattered
remnants of some ancient sculpture or statue. White, presumably marble,
collonades can be glimpsed extending to the south.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
Huge white marble pillars parade to the south. They contrast starkly with the
dark polished marble of the wall itself. The columns abut the darker marble of
the wall but are not a part of it. The columns parade to the south.
name: White Colonnade

desc:
The white marble columns end abruptly at an opening to the south. The darker
marble, however continues its trek to the south and continues into the much
larger room to the south. The white marble columns abut the darker marble of the
walls but are set apart from it. The colonnade continues to the north.
name: White Colonnade

desc:
Exits appear in almost every direction. The archways all meld into one great
pattern, twisting and convoluting uniting two great white arches to the south
and west. The majority of the dome is made up of a dark purple marble with white
striations forking through it. In spots, the marble still looks highly polished,
but centuries of wear and tear have dulled most of its lustre.
name: Large Dome

desc:
Rows of white columns pace this small pathway north to a larger domed area. The
columns are of a stone other than marble and posess none of the flaws that
marble has. The collonade continues to the south.
name: White Colonnade

desc:
The beautifully decorated ceiling is held up by numerous white pillars on each
side of the street. It looks like this part of the mountain is a naturally white
rock, but the dwarves have used rocks and gems from the mines to make the
decorations above.
name: White Colonnade

desc:
This is a circle formed room with exits in two other directions. It is rather
different from the rest of the area. The gold ore coming from the south meets
the white quartz coming from the north. A pillar is standing just in the middle
of the room, where the two types of rock meet. The gold ore and the pale white
quartz are tangled together in a perfect spiral from the floor up to the
ceiling.
name: Golden Crossing

desc:
The road makes a gradual turn here. The outside wall of the tunnel bends
gradually in a fluid motion while the inside wall turns at a right angle. This
gives extra room for wide turns. The road continues to the south and east.
name: A Dark Road

desc:
The walls take on a dull black matte finish here. There is a slight sheen of
dampness on the road as the temperature dips slightly as well. The blackness of
the walls continues on to the west.
name: On a Dark Road

desc:
The road splits into two smaller corridors. A slightly cooler draft wafts in
from the west. The road looks decidedly darker to the west.
name: Road of Narvi

desc:
A very narrow passage has been cut to connect two larger roads. The passage is
very rough and looks quite unfinished. The rough rock gives way to the smoother
more finished rock to the east and west.
name: Narrow Passage

desc:
This is a small room that ascends into a rotunda about thirty feet in height.
The walls are of a dark marble. The rotunda is circular and looks as if it were
placed on top of the four walls of the room. Hollowed out ceiling tiles etched
into intricate patterns make up the bulk of the dome. A small oculus at the peak
of the dome appears darker than the surrounding stone and looks like an air
passage of some kind allowing smoke to escape. There is a staircase recessed
into the west wall leading up and separate tunnels to the north and south.
name: The Old Dome

desc:
Several areas converge here. A wide cobblestone path treks off to the west,
while the south opens up area reaching up to a large dome. There is also a steep
incline going up. Occasionally shafts of light peek down from on high.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
The path continues east and west. Several of the cobblestones are missing making
footing somewhat tricky. Alcoves set into the walls at even intervals extend in
both directions. Most are empty, but a figure of a dwarf looms in one.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
A wide cobblestone path meanders east and west here. The shattered remnants of
several statues are strewn about. Bits of stone bodyparts can be recognized,
with the largest being a full half torso that seems almost alive as it rises out
of the rubble.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
The cobblestone marking the path narrows a little here before widening out again
to the east and west. There is the faintest trace of foetid water trickling in
from the west. The alcoves pacing each side of the path are empty except for the
buildup of a dark green mossy substance.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
The gallery continues in both directions paced by the alcoves on either side.
This section seems to have avoided the water damage but not the smell. The rank
smell of standing water wafts in from the west caressing the nostrils with the
scent of foetid water.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
This simple passage connects two larger paths. The passage itself is completely
bereft of adornment, consisting of two smooth walls.
name: A Passage

desc:
A wide road wrought with wondrous feats of masonry heads south. Intricate
patterns of stone adorn the walls. The stonework is a weaving pattern, not
unlike a painting. Time and damage has taken its toll however, and the story is
tough to decipher. There is a sign carved in a strong dwarven hand. A small
passageway heads off to the east.
name: Road of Narvi

desc:
The road continues in both directions and intersects with an alley which
branches off to the west. There are several indications that a large battle was
fought here some time ago. Scratches on the wall, presumably by the victor,
might have given some insight, but they have been smudged beyond recognition.
name: Road of Narvi

desc:
A wide road built at the height of dwarven masonry. Intricate patterns of stone
adorn both sides of the road. The stonework is a weaving pattern, not unlike a
painting. The elements or something even more base has taken its toll in some
parts.
name: Road of Narvi

desc:
A set of runes carved in Khuzdul on the west wall proclaim this the Road of
Narvi. The road is wide enough for four to walk abreast with no problem and
continues to the north and south.
name: Road of Narvi

desc:
The road ends abruptly here and enter a small circular area to the east. A tall
spire of quartz and gold can be seen rising from the floor to the east. The
flagstones of the road give way to a polished marble surface.
name: Dimrill Road

desc:
The road bends to the east and north. The tunnel makes a slight dip but
recovers. There is collected silt and sediment in the dip as water seems wont to
collect there.
name: Dimrill Road

desc:
This used to once be an inn, but now it is inhabited by something much larger
than a dwarf. The room has been littered with large blocks of granite. The
remains of several corpses are stacked in one of the corners. By the look of the
twisted features and ugly snouts they seem to be orkish.
name: Troll Hall

desc:
This is a small room just off the inn's common room to the west. The thick layer
of dust covering everything in the room is a good indication that whatever moved
the large blocks of granite into the inn to the west doesn't come here.
name: Old Innrooms

desc:
A small plaza has been cut out of the mountain just south of Azaghal's Way. It
was probably used as a place to rest on the long travel through the dark realm.
A large tile is standing up against the southern wall.
name: Small Plaza

desc:
This a small alley which leads off of a large dome to the west. Fluted pilasters
lead right into a ceiling carved with a complex net of interweaving branches.
The overall look is stylized trees forming a canopy of leaves over your head.
name: Durin's Alley

desc:
This small alley leads into a larger road to the east. Fluted pilasters lead
upward to join a mesh of stylized interweaving branches which form the ceiling.
The overall effect is a canopy of trees sloping to a point at the height of the
ceiling. To the south between to pilasters is a large opening.
name: Durin's Alley

desc:
This room was obviously not made by dwarves, but its rough-hewn walls appear to
be fairly old. Dominating the room is a winch-like apparatus, which is ap-
parently used to lift a large block of stone that is currently positioned on the
western wall. On the eastern side of the room is a large pit, and the floor
along the edge is scored with deep grooves. The entire floor near the pit is
stained ruddy red, almost as if the impervious stone had somehow ab- sorbed a
great quantity of blood. On the southern wall is a heavy door made entirely of
black iron, and coated with a dark black substance that flakes to the touch.
name: Rough-hewn Room

desc:
The walls and floor of this enclosed chamber are coated in a thick black
substance that flakes in places revealing a lighter red colour beneath it.
Several rings of metal absolutely caked in the black or red substance have been
driven into the walls at varying heights. There are heavy reinforced doors to
the north and east.
name: Bloody Chamber

desc:
Soupy gore fills the bottom of this pit, varying in depth from two feet along
the northern wall to an unknown depth farther south. Undoubtably the remains of
countless sacrifices have gone into creating this area, an environment that
could not be tolerated by any but the most loathsome of creatures. From time to
time great bubbles slowly rise from the depths, reaching the surface and
bursting with a splattering of red, and ripples of undetermined origin also
occasionally make their way across the surface of the liquid. An almost palpable
malevolence can be felt, as if the presence of any other living crea- ture in
this pit represents an affront that cannot be tolerated.
name: Gore-filled Pit

desc:
Along the northern wall you can see a few statues of the former kings of Moria.
A big tapestry picturing Aule and Durin is also hanging here. The chamber was
astounding in its heyday when the dwarves were here, but that was long ago.
Pieces of rotten flesh, and humanoid bones were carelessly dumped all over the
place. The once so beautiful tapestry is torn and stained with blood, and the
statues have carelessly been toppled over and smashed.
name: Hidden Chamber

desc:
The road ends here. You can see that the intention was to make the road go
further south, but the only exit apart from the road back north is a passage to
the west.
name: Nain's Way

desc:
The road is in very bad repair. Cracks and fissures have occured on the road
surface making travel very difficult. The surface has become almost like steps
as the unstableness has caused one end to lift higher than the other. The walls
have been reinforced haphazardly with wooden beams and stone columns. The
columns look to have been pulled from other parts of Moria as they are a
mismatched set of differing colors and styles.
name: Nain's Way

desc:
The road extends for some distance in both directions. The stonework here is in
good shape - in many places there is clear evidence of repairs having been done
sometime in the relatively recent past.
name: Nain's Way

desc:
This section of the road is in very good repair. None of the flagstones which
comprise the bed of the road are missing or even cracked. The road extends to
the north and south.
name: Nain's Way

desc:
This is an intersection of sorts, with exits in all directions but east. The way
continues to the south but opens up into a larger room to the north. West is a
small passage.
name: Nain's Way

desc:
This is a small chamber which has remained hidden through the generations. There
is a rather large table and a few chairs stacked around it. All the furniture is
still serviceable. The table is piled high with a great many things, covered
with dust and draped with spiderwebs. Some bookshelves line the west wall.
name: Chamber of Zirakzigil
